nodes.label: AI Accelerator Chip
nodes.description: End product — the chip powering AI training and inference
nodes._note: Titan Semiconductor = fictional replacement for TSMC

nodes.label: Advanced Packaging (CoWoS)
nodes.description: Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate — critical bottleneck for AI chip stacking

nodes.label: High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM3e)
nodes.description: Essential high-speed memory layer stacked on AI accelerators — HBM3e fully allocated
nodes._note: Three primary HBM suppliers globally; names withheld per no-trademark rule

nodes.label: EUV Lithography Machine
nodes.description: The $400M machine that makes sub-7nm chips possible — only one supplier globally
nodes._note: Lumen Optics = fictional replacement for ASML. ~50 units/year production capacity. Export-controlled.

nodes.label: Rare Earth Refinery
nodes.description: China controls 98% of gallium, 100% of natural graphite, 100% of dysprosium, ~70% of cobalt refining

nodes.label: Critical Mineral Mine
nodes.description: Raw ore — 17-year average development cycle from discovery to first production
nodes._note: 17 years = ~884 weeks. Timeline lengthening: 13 years avg for late-2000s projects, 18 years for early-2020s. Source: RESOURCES-summary.md.

nodes.label: AI Chip Designer
nodes.description: Fabless semiconductor company designing the accelerator architecture
nodes._note: Meridian Dynamics = fictional replacement for NVIDIA
